title: CAPDAG Capability Definition Schema
description: Schema for capability definitions across all language implementations

oneOf.description: Capability URN with tag-based format (deprecated - use string format)

tags.description: Key-value tags defining the capability. Must include 'in' (input spec ID) and 'out' (output spec ID) as required direction specifiers.

cap_description.description: Human-readable description of what this capability does

documentation.description: Optional long-form markdown documentation. Rendered in capability info panels, the cap navigator, capdag-dot-com, and anywhere else a rich-text explanation of the cap is useful. In TOML sources, author this as a triple-quoted literal string (''') so newlines and markdown punctuation pass through unchanged; the JSON generator re-escapes newlines per JSON rules on output.

media_specs.description: Inline media spec definitions. Each item is a full media spec object with its own urn. Duplicate URNs are not allowed.

args.description: Capability arguments with sources - media_urn is the unique identifier

media_urn.description: Media URN reference (e.g., 'media:object' for built-in, or custom URN from media_specs)

is_sequence.description: Whether this output produces a sequence of items (true) or a single item (false, default)

metadata_json.description: Arbitrary metadata as JSON object for application-specific use

registered_by.description: Registration attribution - who registered this capability and when

username.description: Username of the user who registered this capability

registered_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the capability was registered (format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)

cap_arg.description: Argument definition - media_urn is the unique identifier

media_urn.description: Unique media URN for this argument (e.g., 'media:file-path;textable;scalar')

is_sequence.description: Whether this argument carries a sequence of items (true) or a single item (false, default)

sources.description: How this argument can be provided (stdin, position, cli_flag)

arg_source.description: Source specification for argument input - must have exactly one of: stdin, position, cli_flag
